metaDescription: A self-diagnostic for leadership teams: is your organisation facing symptoms — or causality? Who Aligning Strategy is for.

hero.heading: Symptoms — or causality?
hero.subtitle: A short self-diagnostic for leadership teams

fit.heading: For leadership teams who want a real solution — not a temporary story.

cards.title: Large corporates
cards.body: 4,000–20,000 employees, complex operations, intense competition, material financial stakes.

cards.title: Boards & executive committees
cards.body: Alignment blocked by noise, fear, politics, and unresolved conflict.

cards.title: Private equity
cards.body: Five-year value cycles needing durable correction, not optics.

questions.heading: Is your organisation facing symptoms — or causality?
questions.lead: If several of these land uncomfortably, it usually signals the system — not the people — is constraining performance.

items.title: Competing in a highly contested industry ecosystem
items.sub: Margins are pressured from several directions at once.

items.title: The business model no longer serves the organisation, its people, or shareholders
items.sub: What once worked now produces strain instead of returns.

items.title: High turnover but threatened profitability
items.sub: Activity is high while the financial trajectory is falling.

items.title: The model needs innovation to stay competitive
items.sub: Incremental tweaks are no longer enough to keep pace.

items.title: Scalability of the current operating model is in doubt
items.sub: Growth adds cost and friction faster than it adds value.

items.title: The organisation has moved from growth/maturity into decline
items.sub: The curve has turned and momentum is working against you.

items.title: Senior leaders are under sustained stress
items.sub: Pressure is constant, and the bandwidth to think clearly is shrinking.

items.title: Fear is present in the organisation
items.sub: People protect themselves rather than the enterprise.

items.title: Competitors are circling
items.sub: Dominant incumbents and niche challengers are both applying pressure.

items.title: Repeated failed attempts to shift the organisation
items.sub: Initiatives launch with energy and quietly revert to the old pattern.

items.title: Latent conflict is blocking progress
items.sub: Unspoken tension stalls decisions and execution.

items.title: Considering cosmetic changes to survive
items.sub: Cost cutting, asset sales, or financial engineering are on the table.

callout.title: If several of these resonate
callout.body: It is rarely a people problem. It is usually the system. The next step is a confidential conversation to frame the challenge in causality terms and agree on the few leverage points that matter.
